1.q: The transgenic alfalfa constructed by transforming MtSIN1a showed enhanced salinity tolerance with elevated ROS scavenging.

2.q: The expressions of MtRboh genes and ROS scavenging MtCAT1 were elevated and the enzyme activity of MtCAT1 increased in transgenic plants... demonstrated the positive role of MtLHCB1.4 in salinity tolerance

5.q: MtP5CS1 is reported to encode a developmental 'housekeeping' enzyme, while MtP5CS2 acts as a shoot-specific osmoregulated isoform.

6.q: MtP5CS2 acts as a shoot-specific osmoregulated isoform (Armengaud et al. 2004); expression studied by quantitative real-time RT-PCR in WT and transgenic plants.

11.q: AtUBC32 is an ER stress‐induced functional E2 that localizes to the ER membrane and is involved in ERAD.

12.q: decoding of calcium spiking via a calcium- and calcium/calmodulin-dependent protein kinase (CCaMK)... the central regulator CCaMK

13.q: a number of transcription factors are required for nodule formation, including the CCaMK interaction partner IPD3

19.q: MtGA20ox1 identified among genes involved in GA synthesis upregulated in Mtcir2 mutant; overexpression of GA20ox1-OE rendered transgenic lines greater resistance to salt stress.

20.q: MtGA20ox2 identified among genes involved in GA synthesis upregulated in Mtcir2 mutant; overexpression of GA20ox2-OE rendered transgenic lines greater resistance to salt stress.

22.q: lower levels in MtZPT2-2–overexpressing lines and higher levels in MtZPT2-2-knockout mutants were detected as compared with WT. The results indicated that MtZPT2-2 negatively regulated MtHKT1;1 and MtHKT1;2 expression.

23.q: lower levels in MtZPT2-2–overexpressing lines and higher levels in MtZPT2-2-knockout mutants were detected as compared with WT. The results indicated that MtZPT2-2 negatively regulated MtHKT1;1 and MtHKT1;2 expression.

24.q: In the homozygous lal-2 and lal-3 mutants, the LAL expression was undetectable while the expression levels of MtLHCB1s were significantly higher than those in WT, suggesting a negative regulation of MtLHCBs by LAL.

25.q: The regulatory role of LAL on MtLHCB1.4 is further verified by transient co-expression of LAL and MtLHCB1.4-GFP in tobacco leaves, in which the cleavage of MtLHCB1.4 and production of secondary interfering RNA is identified.

26.q: Consistently increased expression was found for two of these markers: CORA and germin (in MtNAC969 RNAi roots)... expression of salt-stress markers was reduced in over-expressing roots.

27.q: Consistently increased expression was found for two of these markers: CORA and germin (in MtNAC969 RNAi roots); expression of salt-stress markers was reduced in over-expressing roots.

28.q: the expression levels of abiotic stress-responsive genes (MtRD22A and MtCOR15A) and calcium-binding genes (MtCaM and MtCBL4) were upregulated in MtEF1A1 overexpressing lines of M. truncatula.

32.q: We demonstrate that salt stimulation of rhizobium-induced gene expression requires NSP2... NSP2 regulates genes symbiotically hyperinduced by salinity. ENOD11, MtNPL, and DEF expression in roots... in nsp2-2 background in the presence or absence of 100 mM NaCl.

33.q: ENOD11, MtNPL, and DEF expression in roots inoculated with Rm 1021 at 1 dpi in the wild-type or mutant (nsp2-2) background in the presence or absence of 100 mM NaCl. Asterisk (*) indicates a significant difference.

34.q: MtFLS, the gene responsible for flavonol synthase, was significantly downregulated in mtlhy mutants but significantly upregulated in MtLHY-overexpressing plants.

35.q: MtZPT2-2 was demonstrated to bind with the promoter of MtHKT1;1 and MtHKT1;2 to inhibit their expression.

36.q: MtZPT2-2 was demonstrated to bind with the promoter of MtHKT1;1 and MtHKT1;2 to inhibit their expression.

37.q: A yeast one-hybrid (Y1H) assay showed that the MtLHY protein binds to the promoter sequences of MtFLS; A dual luciferase assay showed MtLHY increased the expression of LUC driven by the promoter of MtFLS.

38.q: Overexpression of MtZpt2-1 in roots conferred salt tolerance and affected the expression of three putative targets in the predicted manner: a cold-regulated A (CORA) homolog, a flower-promoting factor (FPF1) homolog and an auxin-induced proline-rich protein (PRP) gene.

39.q: a homolog of the abiotic stress-related COLD-REGULATEDA1 gene and a TFIIIA-related transcription factor (TF), MtZpt2-1, known to regulate the former gene.

40.q: CAS31 reduced Na+ accumulation by down-regulating HKT1 expression under salt stress.

41.q: NO fluorescence was clearly detected in transgenic lines compared to wild type, suggesting that MtSAP1 overexpressing seedlings produced more NO than wild type.

42.q: Higher levels of Na+ in MtZPT2-2–overexpressing lines, but lower levels in MtZPT2-2–knockout mutants were observed as compared with WT after salt treatment. The results indicated that MtZPT2-2 expression affected Na+ homeostasis.

43.q: MtCIR2-dependent seed germination under salt stress was accounted for by a decrease in the endogenous GA concentration through silencing of GA biosynthesis gene GA20ox1/2.

44.q: RNA pull-down assays and LC–MS/MS using MtCIR2 as a probe labeled with biotin showed MtCIR2 specifically interacted with BMI1; TriFC assay confirmed MtCIR2 was associated with BMI1 in the nucleus.

45.q: Two P5CS genes have been isolated from M. truncatula: MtP5CS1 … MtP5CS2 acts as a shoot-specific osmoregulated isoform.

46.q: Five genes of MtRCI2(A–E) showed a high similarity to MsRCI2A in the Medicago truncatula genome.

47.q: Five genes of MtRCI2(A–E) showed a high similarity to MsRCI2A in the Medicago truncatula genome.

48.q: NSP2, which functions as a node to integrate the abiotic and biotic signals... salt stimulation of rhizobium-induced gene expression requires NSP2.

49.q: Two Srlk-TILLING mutants also failed to limit root growth in response to salt stress and accumulated fewer sodium ions than controls.

50.q: MtABI5, a central component of the ABA signaling cascade.

51.q: enhanced H2A ubiquitination at the loci encoding ABA catabolic enzyme gene CYP707A2 and GA biosynthesis gene GA20ox1/2.

52.q: the Na+/K+ ratio in the leaves of MtLHY-overexpressing lines was lower than that in WT, suggesting that MtLHY mediates Na+/K+ homeostasis in response to salt stress.

53.q: The Western blot assay showed that the protein translation of MtTCP4 was suppressed by Mtr-miR319a.

55.q: Significant increases in mRNA levels of CYP707A2 in the Mtcir2 mutant; significant decreases in overexpression lines OE-8 and OE-9, verifying CYP707A2 is a downstream target of MtCIR2.
